ops.title: com.cc-steer.watch crash-loop root cause: unpinned uvx resolution missing mlx_lm (791/804 launches)
ops.body: Recon (2026-07-18, sonnet lane in wf_0c49d41e-ef9, evidence ~/.cc-steer/logs/watch.log Jul 8–17): 791 of 804 logged launches of `com.cc-steer.watch` died seconds after exec with `RuntimeError: the local watcher requires the 'mlx' extra` from `_mlx_lm()` (cc_steer/watcher/drafter_mlx.py:186-192), raised eagerly in `MlxDrafter.__init__` at daemon start. Root cause: per-launch `uvx --from 'cc-steer[gate,mlx]'` ephemeral resolution served an env missing mlx_lm ~98.5% of the time — an UNPINNED spec re-resolved across 9.5 days of active releases, with three sibling launchd agents (watch: `[gate,mlx]`, pipeline: bare, retrain: `[retrain]`) resolving the same unpinned name against the shared ~/.cache/uv tool cache. NOT the cause: ccp env stderr noise ("no terminal session leader" — harmless, ccp's stdout is only CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR exports); OOM/Jetsam (zero matches); missing model files. A separate one-time crash (`RuntimeError: There is no Stream(cpu, 0) in current thread` — MLX thread-affinity via anyio.to_thread) is ALREADY FIXED in current source (draft() calls decide() synchronously, drafter_mlx.py:88-100) — do not re-fix or reintroduce the thread hop. Fix shipped in cc-steer v0.11.0 Phase B: version-pin the uvx --from spec in all three launchd command builders (embed installed version at install-launchd time, cc_steer/launchd.py watch_prefix ~:149) + lazy drafter load. The watcher was UP at recon time (23.5h, mirror-mode silent — a quiet watch.log is not evidence of a hang).
